2 Ways To Quickly Build A Fat
Em.ail List ... UsingJust Free Em.ail
Dear Subscriber,
The big secret everyone wants to know is list building. "Ben, how can I build my
email list so I can make even more smackeroos using these email secrets
you're revealing???" That's the ~iterally) million dollar question, ain't it? It's a GOOD
question, too. Mter all, if you don't have a list you're actively growing, then sending out
emails will make you about as much money as going to a bank you don't have an account
with. So today I'm going to show you two powerful ways (I'm using myself) to generate
pre-sold traffic and build my list. Hey, I'm the same as you -- I am always needing to feed
and grow my list or I'm as dead in the water as the other "list-less" ones. And, here's the
cool part: Neither of these ways is complicated or require you to spend any money
whatsoever. In fact, they are both based on ... you guessed it... sending email! Yes, my
friend, I'm going to show you how to generate "ready to subscribe" traffic (i.e. they go to
your site LOOKING to subscribe to your list) using JUST simple emails. In fact, just to
make this brain-dead easy on your righteous self, I'm even handing you exact templates
you can use for your product/service/goals. Almost no thinking is required. I've pretty
much done the thinking for you. All you do is turn on your computer, swap out my info
with yours, and push "send." I don't know how to make it any easier than that!
So, without further ado, let's get. it. awwwwn ....

The first way is by sending emails to other people (big "players" or peers, more on
this in a second ... ) in your niche with the goal of them mailing your offer or website link
to their list. This is one of the fastest ways to build your list full of qualified subscribers
ever invented. One endorsed mailing by the right list owner or just one of your articles on
their site (a great way to doJV's) can mean 100+ new subscribers on yc oldc list in 24
hours. I know this because I've done it multiple times. I'd say a good 25% of my list was
installed not by my own devices, but by a client who plugged me a couple times to his list.
Another big chunk (at least 300-400 net subscribers) came JUST from writing an article
on Copyblogger.com and being interviewed on their podcast. In those cases they
approached me. But you can get in with the same types of sites and people if you usc
email the way you're about to see. And by the way, while email may not be the most
reliable way to connect (direct mail and other offline gimmicks work much better), it can
still get the job done. (And it's free, so what do you have to lose?) To kick things off, I'm
going to show you -- word-for-word -- the exact email my friend David Dutton used to go
from a broke nobody selling space heaters out of a pickup truck in college... to being an
extremely successful marketing consultant who has joint ventured with guys like David
Frey,Joe Vitale,Jeff Walker, Eban Pagan (who he works with now ongoing),Jim Straw
(mail order legend), the guy who was pronounced "The World's Strongest Man" (forget
his name) and even one or two people who were on Donald Trumps' "The Apprentice."
Actually, he's joint ventured with many more people than this, I've just lost track. The
following email is what he used to connect with these most of these people. And that
email turned intoJV's and also other opportunities that let him rapidly build his list. (And
by the way, you can -- and should -- read more about David Dutton at
www.WholsDavidDutton.com.)
His entire career started with this email he gave me permission to reprint.
Figure what I'll do now is show you this email (it's very pithy and non-complicated,
just the way ALL emails should be). And then we'll break it down afterwards so you can
easily tweak and adapt it for YOUR purposes/product/service/goals/situation. Okay,
here it is ...

Hey John,

My but that was short and sweet and to the point, wasn't it? Hardly looks very
"sexy'' at all, does it? No, it doesn't. In fact, it looks like something a person would send to
another person without using any sales letter, copywriting or "persuasion" tricks at all.
Yet, it laid the foundation for his entire business. But, that doesn't mean there isn't a lot
going on in this bad-boy. Let's break this down and see what makes it "tick." These are,
by the way, the same things that make ALL great emails tick (even emails you send to your
list every day -- you DO send emails to your list each day, right???)
Respects The Reader's Tim.e
First, it's short and to the point. Dave doesn't waste their time trying to dazzle
them with how clever he is or anything like that. It gets in and gets out as quickly and
efficiendy as possible. I have no scientific proof or test results to back this up, but my
opinion is most emails are deleted first and foremost because they don't respect the
reader's time. (Too long winded, don't get to the point, etc.)
Warm And Friendly
This is a very friendly email. He's not trying to pretend he's something he's not or
have an attitude to make himself look "tough" (something people in the Internet
marketing world, at least, are weary of. .. ) Even the subject line is clear and transparent --
he's not trying to trick you into reading it, he really DOES have a "quick question."
No Hidden Agenda
Dave has no agenda with this other than to connect (he ain't called "the most

connected man on the Internet" for nuttin'). He's not asking anyone to read his 300 page
book or mail his offer to their list or "take a quick look" at his newest blog post. (Again,
doing such things shows zero respect for someone's time ...)
Social Proof
If there's a "technique" being used in this email it's the social proof. We marketing
types can see it a mile away. But, in this sense, it does not look or feel like a technique or
tool he's using to gain leverage (that's the effect of it, but not the purpose). This is the
second most powerful component of this email -- the social proof. The people he was
sending it to (and he likely switched the names out with others as time went on, this is the
original email he sent, it's been tweaked since) all knew the names mentioned. It makes
Dave very "safe" to listen to. Very important.
Phone Nmnber
According to Dave, his phone number is the real backbone of this email. The
social proof is important, but he said it was including his phone number that really sealed
the deal. For one thing, nobody else online does it. And for another, nobody else online
does it! And what would happen is, some (not all) of the people he sent it to ... called him
back. Imagine that! To paraphrase copywriter John Francis Tighe -- "in the land of
e~nails, the dude who still uses the phone is king."
Now, did this work every time he sent it out?
or course not.
But it worked a LOT more than you might realize. A big reason for this is he chose
WHO he contacted very carefully. He didn't just load his email program up with
everyone and anyone and throw caution to the wind and push "send." No, he found
people whose products he'd bought and who (as he told me) he was a fan of already. That
made his email I 00% genuine, warm and safe to spend time reading. How can you not at
least read an email from a genuine fan of your products? You can do the same thing. Is
there someone who you want to meet in business? A list you want to mail? Someone you
want to joint venture with? Then take Dave's email above and tweak it for your purposes.
You can make your own version, too, just make sure the above elements are in it. And
don't think they have to be "big names", either. One way to use an email like this is find

your ~ and send to them. So what if they only have 500 people on their list? You get
enough small list owners to mail for you and you can rapidly build your list. They don't all
have to be gurus. The key is to connect with the111 with no agenda. Get to know
people, first. Don't just start asking them to do you favors. Actually... befriend people.
Especially bigger list owners. See if there's something you can do for them first, then later
on (it takes time) see aboutJV opportunities. For peers, you can be a bit more aggressive.
If, for example, you have 300 people on your list, find other people with 200-300 people --
who sell similar but not competing products-- on their list and send the above email.
Connect with them. You can build your list VERY rapidly that way without talking to a
single guru in your niche. By the way. .. a lot of people "poo-poo" on using email to
connect with others. They will tell you email has very little impact, that busy people are
more likely to ignore your email and that you are MUCH better off using a real letter or
FedEx, etc. And they are right! Email DOES have small impact compared to the above.
But here's the thing: What I like to do is use email FIRST and then, if that doesn't work,
call in the big gun (offline) methods. Some other ways to get attention (should email fail)
include: Sending a telegram (who could ignore a telegram???), sending a ball with your
message (another David Dutton classic, go to sendaball.com), a fake newspaper clipping
(my friend Doberman Dan did this to connect with the late, great Gary Halbert.) Anyway,
your homework is to find 5-10 people you want to connect with and use the above email
template to contact them. See what happens. Maybe nothing will. But maybe ... just
maybe ... it'll turn into a wonderful new joint venture and a bigger list for you.
Okay that's the first way to use plain, simple little cmails to build your list.

Okay, enough build up. Here's what we're gonna do: First, I'm going to give you

Gust check through their site archives under your category) or maybe just a few. But

Critique Time!
Below is an email for a weight loss product. My comments are in the bold
[bracketed] parts:
I 	get a lot of questions asking me how to get rid of cellulite.
So I'll answer this today so you'll have 1 less thing to worry
about while losing weight. 	[Beautiful opening. Answers a question
people have and gets IMMEDIATE attention, 	it's hard deleting an
email like this]
First, 	the cottage cheese dimpling of cellulite is caused by an
uneven fat distribution within the fat cells due to poor
circulation and localized buildup of lymph in the lymphatic
system.
Diet, 	exercise and weight loss do help a little bit since they
cause your fat cells to shrink- hence, 	the dimpling effect can
shrink. But the cellulite won't go away just because of weight
loss. 	[hmmm ••• 	I didn't know that ••• ]
Even skinny people have cellulite. 	[Really???]
Expensive cellulite creams that claim they are fat dissolvers
obviously don't work. 	If exercise and losing weight can't
completely get rid of cellulite, why do people think a umagic
cream" would! 	[Superb job of disqualifying the competition!
Something few people do in their emails]

Okay, this email started out perfectly. It got right in sync with the customers and
educated them about the problem (not the solution). Then, it made the classic email
blunder by starting to hard teach when it got into the foam roller. Bad move. Especially if
you teach the foam roller in your book. In fact I wouldn't have talked about the foam
roller at all. I would have talked about cellulite more. So you educate them on what
cellulite (the problem) is, how it grows, why women get it, etc. (which you did a fantastic
job oQ. They are getting interesting info that is useful, but it's not revealing what's in your
paid product. Then, at the end you can say something like:
"The bad news is, 	it's almost impossible to get rid of cellulite
if you follow mainstream instruction. Believe me, 	I tried all the
tips and it rarely works unless you are super disciplined and
have a lot of money to spend. The GOOD news is there are two
other ways -- both simple and way cheaper than creams -- for
eliminating that stubborn cellulite in as little as 30-60 days.
To see how I did it recently go to [PLUG PRODUCT]."
